session_label: Avenue 1 Caucasus re-probe with corrected canonical URLs (main-context, VPN-off)
scope: Re-probe of Caucasus institutional manuscript portals after prior-round flagged all 4 as network-blocked. This session discovered current canonical URL hierarchy via WebSearch and re-probed under VPN-off direct US/UK routing. Goal: defensible reachability status for each portal + verify whether scholarly-trail Hagigi witness references map to existing v1.0 register entries.
geopolitical_caveat: Iranian wartime intermittent shutdowns do not affect Az/Geo/Am portals. VPN was OFF during this session; results reflect direct routing.

method_summary.tier_1_url_discovery: WebSearch for current canonical hostnames of AMEA Institute of Manuscripts, Azerbaijan National Library, Georgian National Centre of Manuscripts, Matenadaran. Findings cross-checked against institutional Wikipedia entries and Al-Furqan Islamic Heritage Foundation digital-library registry.
method_summary.tier_2_reachability: WebFetch each canonical URL; capture HTTP status, body length, search-form presence, language of interface.
method_summary.tier_3_scholarly_trail_disambiguation: WebSearch + WebFetch Azerbaijani-language popular-history articles (azertag.az, azadliq.info) that recount the Hagigi divan provenance chain. Cross-check named MS locations against v1.0 witness_register.json.

reachability.vpn_on_prior_round: socket-closed-unexpectedly (likely UA-block under VPN routing); not ECONNREFUSED
reachability.search_interface: static landing page exposes search-form action /index.php?do=search; e-catalog at manuscript.az/ek/ reachable but search backend JS-rendered; full search would need Playwright

queries_executed.outcome: static catalog index lists personal-library catalogues; no Hagigi-specific catalogue surfaced in this page
search interface JS-rendered; static body shows no result records

Azerbaijani-language electronic catalog landing; advertises 'axtarış aparmaq mümkündür' across old printed books (4427 items) + research publications (1950-2023); search backend not exposed in static HTML

scholarly_trail_mentions_on_portal.main_page_acquisition_log: AMEA newly-acquired-manuscripts table mentions 'Jahanshah Hagigi (1405-1467) as an Azerbaijan poet and ruler whose divan manuscript was obtained from Istanbul Suleymaniye Library'. The dates 1405-1467 confirm Cihanşah Qaraqoyunlu (not Yusuf Hakîkî Bursavi d.1487).
scholarly_trail_mentions_on_portal.interpretation: The acquisition is a copy / microfilm / facsimile of a Süleymaniye-held Hagigi divan; the most likely source codex is suleymaniye_fatih_3808 (already in v1.0 register, scribe-date 20th c.). The AMEA acquisition does NOT constitute a new institutional witness; it is an AMEA-held copy of an existing register entry.

portals.verdict: REACHABLE_NO_NEW_WITNESS; AMEA acquisition is a copy of suleymaniye_fatih_3808 (v1.0 register entry)

reachability.anl_az_legacy: REACHABLE; HTTP 200; Azerbaijani / English bilingual interface
reachability.ek_anl_az_e_catalog: CERT_ERROR ('unable to verify the first certificate') consistent across VPN-on and VPN-off; server-side cert chain issue, not network block
reachability.verdict: REACHABLE_LANDINGS; e-catalog backend cert-walled to WebFetch

queries_executed.outcome: no Cihansah / Hakiki / Hagigi / Karakoyunlu references in static landing HTML
e-catalog URL pattern confirmed http://ek.anl.az/search/query?theme=e-kataloq but server returns cert error; would need Playwright with cert-ignore for query execution

portals.verdict: REACHABLE_LANDING_CERT_WALLED_BACKEND; defer to Playwright cert-ignore pass; low expected yield (ANL Az holdings overlap with AMEA Institute of Manuscripts which is the primary Az institutional witness surface)

portals.portal_name: Georgian National Centre of Manuscripts (Kekelidze Institute)

reachability.vpn_off_this_session: REACHABLE_TEST_MODE; HTTP 200; Georgian-language interface; site displays 'ვებგვერდი მუშაობს სატესტო რეჟიმში' (site operates in test mode)
reachability.search_interface: Search icon visible in navigation but no explicit search-form action URL surfaced from static HTML; collection categories listed including 'აღმოსავლური ხელნაწერების ფონდი' (Oriental Manuscripts Fund); no Persian-specific browse

queries_executed.query: landing page browse + Oriental Manuscripts Fund link discovery
queries_executed.outcome: Oriental Manuscripts Fund link present but Persian-specific URL not surfaced; site is test mode

portals.scope_finding: Per prior-round Kekelidze investigation and EAP1526 documentation, the Georgian National Centre of Manuscripts' Persian holdings center on Afshar/Safavid period 1736-1796 documents, post-dating Cihanşah Qaraqoyunlu (d. 1467). Scope mismatch. EAP1526 ('Persian and Georgian-Persian historical documents of Afshar period 1736-1796 in the Georgian National Centre of Manuscripts') confirmed via WebSearch this round.
portals.verdict: REACHABLE_NO_NEW_WITNESS_SCOPE_MISMATCH; collection is post-Cihanşah Afshar/Safavid period

portals.portal_name: Matenadaran (Mesrop Mashtots Institute of Ancient Manuscripts, Yerevan)

reachability.vpn_off_this_session: REACHABLE; HTTP 200; English interface; grand catalogue advertised
reachability.search_interface: Grand catalogue page describes the collection but no online search-form action URL surfaced from static HTML; per subagent B investigation, Matenadaran has no online search; canonical Persian catalogue is Kostikyan 2017 paywalled Academia.edu

scholarly_trail_disambiguation.popular_history_reference: Azerbaijani-language popular-history accounts (Az Wikipedia + Azadliq.info + Azertag.az) describe an Hagigi divan bequeathed by NYC Armenian collector Arutyun Gazaryan to 'Yerevan University', discovered there by Lətif Hüseynzadə who published it (Yerevan 1966 edition).
scholarly_trail_disambiguation.subagent_B_triangulation_outcome: The Az Wiki narrative CONFLATES BL Or 9493's London examination with the Yerevan-held codex. Hüseynzadə was at Yerevan Pedagogical Institute (not YSU); discovery 1961; first publication Yerevan 1966. The Gazaryan-bequest codex is most plausibly Matenadaran MS 965 itself, via the 1959 YSU-to-Matenadaran institutional consolidation documented in Iranica MATENADARAN.
scholarly_trail_disambiguation.v1.0_register_mapping: matenadaran_965 (879 AH / 1474-75 CE, 54 ff., complete divan). Existing scholarly_attestation block already cites Hüseyinzade 1966 Yerevan publication and Rahimov/Hüseyinzade/Aliyev 1986/2006 Bakı edition. The Gazaryan provenance and 1959 consolidation are augmentation candidates for the existing entry; no new witness.

portals.verdict: REACHABLE_NO_NEW_WITNESS; matenadaran_965 already in v1.0 register; Gazaryan/Hüseyinzade provenance augmentation pending

reachability.search_endpoint_GET: HTTP_405_METHOD_NOT_ALLOWED for GET requests on /search?query=<term>; search interface uses POST or JS
reachability.search_interface: Cannot exercise via WebFetch GET; needs Playwright form submission OR direct EAP project-page enumeration

relevant_projects_surfaced_via_websearch.title: Persian and Georgian-Persian historical documents of Afshar period (1736-1796) in the Georgian National Centre of Manuscripts
relevant_projects_surfaced_via_websearch.scope_assessment: POST-CIHANSAH; Afshar period 1736-1796 is 269-329 years after Cihanşah's death; out of v1.0 poetry scope; potentially relevant for v2.0 chancery-document scope as parallel to asnad.org corpus

relevant_projects_surfaced_via_websearch.title: Georgian national heritage materials at the National Scientific Library
relevant_projects_surfaced_via_websearch.scope_assessment: Georgian-language scientific materials; not Persian manuscripts

portals.verdict: PROBED_NEGATIVE_VIA_WEBSEARCH; no EAP project digitizes a 15th-century Cihansah-era Persian-Turkic poetry corpus

portals.portal_name: Dagestan Gamzat Tsadasa Institute (Russian Academy of Sciences branch, Makhachkala)
portals.scope_finding: Per Encyclopaedia Iranica DAGHESTAN entry: Gamzat Tsadasa Institute holdings comprise 2678 manuscripts (2637 Arabic, 16 Turkic, 3 Persian, balance in Dagestani languages). Only 3 Persian MSS total. Microscopic surface for Persian poetry; even if all 3 were probed, the prior probability of a Cihansah witness is near-zero given the broader pattern (Cihansah-attributed MSS cluster in Istanbul / London / Tehran / Yerevan, not Dagestan).
portals.verdict: OUT_OF_SCOPE_MICROSCOPIC_SURFACE; only 3 Persian MSS in entire institute

hagigi_provenance_chain_v1_register_mapp.purpose: Az-language popular-history accounts (Az Wikipedia + Azadliq.info + Azertag.az 2012) outline a 6-location provenance narrative for Cihansah Hagigi's divan. This block maps each named location onto v1.0 register entries and confirms no new witnesses.

popular_history_locations.location: Istanbul Topkapi/Suleymaniye library (until Abdülhamid era, then lost)
popular_history_locations.v1_register_mapping: Possibly the source codex for suleymaniye_fatih_3808 (20th-c. copy); the originals 'lost in Abdülhamid era' may have been dispersed/destroyed/relocated
Not surfaced as extant; possibly the predecessor of the London codex (intermediate transit point)
bl_or_9493 (1488 CE, Isfahani scribe, examined by Minorsky 1954)

popular_history_locations.location: New York Arutyun Gazaryan private archive (post-London, bequeathed onward)
popular_history_locations.v1_register_mapping: Provenance chain only; the bequeathed codex is most plausibly the Matenadaran 965 itself per subagent B triangulation

popular_history_locations.location: Yerevan University, found by Hüseynzadə, published Yerevan 1966
popular_history_locations.v1_register_mapping: matenadaran_965 (Hüseyinzade 1966 already cited in scholarly_attestation; 1959 YSU-to-Matenadaran consolidation makes 'Yerevan University' and 'Matenadaran' the same codex at different institutional moments)
tehran_university_8198 (Alemdârî 2001 publication; AzerTAC 2012 'autograph claim' already documented and REFUTED in v1.0 iran_caucasus_sweep_findings block)

hagigi_provenance_chain_v1_register_mapp.net_outcome: All 6 popular-history locations either map onto existing v1.0 register entries (4 of 6) or are dispersal/intermediate-transit points without extant witness (2 of 6: Istanbul-original and Alexandria). Zero new witnesses surfaced.

summary.v1_register_impact: ZERO new witnesses. matenadaran_965 entry recommended for augmentation with Gazaryan provenance + 1959 YSU-Matenadaran consolidation caveat + Hüseyinzadə institutional affiliation clarification (Pedagogical Institute not YSU).
summary.v2_chancery_scope_findings: EAP1526 (Persian and Georgian-Persian Afshar 1736-1796 documents at GNCM) recommended as v2.0 chancery-scope parallel to asnad.org Cihansah corpus.
methodological_findings: VPN-on/off flip changed reachability signature for manuscript.az (socket-closed when VPN-routed; HTTP 200 when direct) and manuscript.ge (ECONNREFUSED when VPN-routed; reachable test-mode when direct). Documents that geo-IP routing via VPN can introduce false-negative blockage signatures.

Newly-acquired-manuscripts log on AMEA manuscript.az main page is an under-exploited probe surface: institutional acquisition tables reveal source-library shelfmarks that link to existing register entries.

Popular-history Azerbaijani-language provenance accounts (Az Wikipedia + Azertag.az) conflate institutional consolidation events (1959 YSU-Matenadaran transfer) and can fabricate apparent 'multiple witnesses' from a single codex at different institutional moments.
EAP search endpoint requires POST or JS form submission; GET requests return HTTP 405.
